English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Henry Andrews Babcock papers
Babcock, Henry Andrews, 1891-
3022
11 cubic feet. 11 cubic feet.
1924-1961. Pamphlets, certificates, reports, and other documents. Valuation, appraisal and audit reports, certificates and summaries of William H. Babcock and Sons, Inc., 1924-1936. Also, Babcock's files of the Santa Monica, California and Bunker Hill, Los Angeles Redevelopment Projects; and material concerning mass transportation, primarily in Los Angeles. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 11 12 1 ======= 11 12 1 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2067293
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC staff
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, March 2002
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
A real estate appraiser and engineer, and a former partner of William H. Babcock and Sons, Real Estate Consultants and Valuators of Chicago, Babcock also taught finance courses at the University of Southern California and UCLA. He was involved in several studies of urban transportation problems and mass transit development, primarily in Los Angeles, California. Member, International Fraternity of Lambda Alpha, Los Angeles Chapter.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Valuation, appraisal and audit reports, certificates and summaries of William H. Babcock and Sons, Inc., 1924-1936. Also, Babcock's files of the Santa Monica, California and Bunker Hill, Los Angeles Redevelopment Projects; material concerning mass transportation, primarily in Los Angeles; pamphlets and reports.
RELATED MATERIAL
Part of the Lambda Alpha Literary Archives.
Cite As:
Henry Andrews Babcock Papers, #3022. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Los Angeles (Calif.) Santa Monica (Calif.) Local transit -- California -- Los Angeles. College teachers -- United States. Real estate appraisers. Engineers. Urban transportation -- California -- Los Angeles. Real property -- Valuation. City planning. William H. Babcock and Sons Lambda Alpha International. Los Angeles Chapter
